name: Cajun Catfish with Spicy Strawberry Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Strawberry-flavored vinegar may be used instead of red wine vinegar. Place fillets in a large shallow dish.
2. Season the fish with salt, black pepper, and hot pepper sauce; cover and refrigerate for 1 hour.
3. In a small saucepan, combine preserves, vinegar, soy sauce, seafood cocktail sauce, minced garlic, and horseradish. Simmer the sauce over low heat, stirring occasionally while preparing the catfish.
4. Blend cornmeal and flour in a shallow bowl.
5. Drain the catfish and dredge it in the cornmeal mixture, coating all sides.
6. Heat oil in a heavy skillet over medium-high heat. When hot, add the catfish and sauté until browned on both sides. Drain well on paper towels; keep warm.
7. Spoon 1/4 cup of sauce on each plate; top with catfish fillets. Garnish with sliced strawberries and parsley if desired.
